mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in industrial production reflect real economic expansion which supports corporate earnings and equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rolling 2-year Rank IC of INDPRO on 60-day returns remains below 0.05, or 60-day returns after positive INDPRO surprises are not significantly positive (t-test p>0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate above the natural rate signals weakening labor demand and impending recession, which reduces expected earnings and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: When UNRATE is above 4.5%, forward 20-day S&P 500 returns are positive in more than 60% of instances, or the average return is not significantly negative (t-test p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Reserve raises the federal funds rate while inflation remains elevated, it indicates tightening monetary policy to curb demand, which reduces equity valuations through higher discount rates and lower earnings expectations, leading to negative short-term returns.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases by at least 25bp over the prior 3 months and CPIAUCSL is above 4% year-over-year
failure_condition: In periods where FEDFUNDS is rising and CPI is above 4% YoY, 20-day forward returns are not materially negative (median return > -1%) or the interaction coefficient in a linear model is not significantly negative (p>0.10).

mechanism_narrative: In late-cycle expansion phases characterized by low unemployment and robust payroll gains, further increases in nonfarm payrolls signal overheating and labor market tightness that compresses profit margins and accelerates Fed tightening, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE below 4% and PAYEMS 3-month average change > 200k
failure_condition: During late-cycle regime (UNRATE<4%, payroll change>200k), 20-day forward returns are not significantly negative (t-test p>0.05), or the regime indicator fails to differentiate returns from other periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month decline in real retail and food services sales signals abrupt consumer spending contraction, which directly reduces corporate revenue expectations and triggers negative equity returns over the following week.
failure_condition: When RSAFS declines more than 2% MoM, forward 5-day S&P 500 returns are not significantly negative (t-test p>0.05), or the sharpe ratio of the short signal is less than 0.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in housing starts and industrial production indicate coordinated demand-side expansion that boosts cyclical sectors and overall equity market, leading to positive short-term returns.
candidate_trigger: Both HOUST and INDPRO have positive 1-month growth
failure_condition: When both HOUST and INDPRO are growing, forward 5-day returns are not significantly positive (t-test p>0.05), or the joint signal has no predictive power beyond individual series.

mechanism_narrative: During post-recession recovery periods, GDP growth accelerates from a depressed base, and rising GDP releases positive earnings surprises that drive sustained equity gains over the following quarter.
failure_condition: In periods identified as post-recession recovery (NBER trough within prior 12 months), 60-day forward returns are not significantly higher than unconditional mean (t-test p>0.05), or GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth does not correlate with subsequent returns.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds the neutral rate estimate, further rate increases become more damaging to equity valuations as they tighten financial conditions beyond a normal level, leading to negative returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 5%, 60-day forward returns are not significantly negative (t-test p>0.05), or the negative return frequency is less than 55%.
